selected_article.title: Enhancing Public Healthcare Delivery through Digital Innovation: Policy and Practical Outcomes

current_article_analysis.title_detected: Enhancing Public Healthcare Delivery through Digital Innovation: Policy and Practical Outcomes
current_article_analysis.meta_description_preview: This article clarifies the intersection of policy frameworks and technological adoption, assessing how digital innovation can improve accessibility and efficiency in public healthcare.

upgrade_hypothesis.core_upgrade_goal: Convert the selected article into a stronger Drishvara long-form policy insight piece without changing the article file in AG08C.
upgrade_hypothesis.reader_value_goal: Help readers understand how digital innovation can improve public healthcare delivery beyond technology adoption, focusing on governance, service reliability, data use, accountability and citizen experience.
upgrade_hypothesis.proposed_reader_promise: A clearer, practical explanation of why digital health systems succeed only when technology, workflow, field capacity and accountability move together.
upgrade_hypothesis.proposed_angle: Digital health innovation as a governance and delivery-strengthening instrument, not merely a software or dashboard exercise.

proposed_structure.heading: Why digital innovation matters in public healthcare delivery
proposed_structure.purpose: Set up the governance and service-delivery context.

proposed_structure.heading: From technology adoption to service improvement
proposed_structure.purpose: Distinguish tools from outcomes and explain why implementation quality matters.

proposed_structure.heading: Data systems, accountability and frontline workflows
proposed_structure.purpose: Explain how dashboards, records and alerts become useful only when tied to field workflows.

proposed_structure.heading: Digital inclusion and patient trust
proposed_structure.purpose: Cover citizen experience, access, language, grievance channels and last-mile usability.

proposed_structure.heading: Risks: fragmentation, data quality and over-centralisation
proposed_structure.purpose: Record balanced risks and implementation safeguards.

proposed_structure.heading: What a mature digital-health delivery model should track
proposed_structure.purpose: Introduce useful indicators and practical monitoring dimensions.

proposed_structure.heading: Reader’s lens
proposed_structure.purpose: End with a practical interpretation for policy readers and administrators.

candidate_reference_themes: Digital health mission / national digital health architecture
Public health system strengthening and service delivery
Health information systems and data quality
Telemedicine or digital access in public healthcare
Governance/accountability frameworks for health service delivery

visual_data_need_plan.recommended_visual_concept: Patient need → digital record/triage → facility workflow → referral/medicine/service → feedback/accountability loop

ag08d_handoff.next_stage_title: Inference, Reference and Visual Readiness Review
ag08d_handoff.allowed_scope: review candidate packet, create inference/readiness review and identify gaps
